items.title: Winners, losers from Lu Dort trade to Atlanta in three-team deal
items.summary: It's not unexpected that Lu Dort got traded this offseason.Oklahoma City felt the pressure of the second apron closing in as the max extensions for Chet Holmgren and Jalen Williams kicked in for next season, and some depth was going to be sacrificed. Isaiah Joe and Aaron Wiggins were already gone, but Dort is making $18.2 million next season and is up for an extension the Thunder couldn't swing, so the sides were always likely to part.Atlanta stepped up, Dallas jumped in, and we have a three-team trade. Who are the winners and losers? Let's break it all down, starting with a reminder of the trade itself:Oklahoma City receives: Three future second-round picksAtlanta receives: Luguentz Dort, Ryan NembhardDallas receives: Zaccharie RisacherWinner: Atlanta HawksThis just adds to what has been a strong offseason for the Hawks — they got the best player in this trade.Atlanta is…

Nembhard joins fellow Zag wing Corey Kispert in Atlanta,…

items.title: Yaxel Lendeborg outduels Cameron Boozer to lead Warriors past Grizzlies for NBA Summer League championship
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors selected Yaxel Lendeborg with the No. 11 pick in the NBA Draft hoping that the 23-year-old rookie with an NCAA title from Michigan is ready to contribute right away.He looked the part Sunday night while leading the Warriors to a 94-90 win over the Grizzles in the NBA Summer League championship game in Las Vegas that featured a high-profile matchup against against Memphis rookie Cameron Boozer.The game was tied at 47 at halftime. Boozer exploded for 10 of his 19 points in the third quarter, including a 3-pointer in the final seconds to extend a Grizzlies lead to 76-66.Cameron Boozer sinks the 3 at the end of Q3 🎯The 3rd overall pick has a game-high 17 PTS!Memphis leads by 10 heading into Q4 of the NBA Summer League Championship. pic.twitter.com/8uJhg3OQCY— NBA (@NBA) July 20, 2026But the fourth quarter belonged to Lendeborg, who scored seven straight Warriors…

items.title: NBA Offseason Trade/Free Agent Rumors 2026: Stars make their LeBron pitches, Klay Thompson available
items.summary: While we saw a trade on Sunday, it feels like NBA free agency is on pause waiting for LeBron James to make his free agency decision, which will come... eventually. Everyone — the front offices involved, other players, fans — is ready for LeBron to end his dragged-out free agency process, but he will do it on his own time. Until then, stars keep making their pitches, but other free agency rumors are flying around. Here are some of the latest.Harden, Edwards make LeBron pitchesAmong the most predictable, tedious, frankly clickbait things about LeBron's free agency has been players, coaches and front-office people from every team that might land him — and a few that haven't — being asked about him and asked to make their public pitches. The fact that 76ers owner Josh Harris had a quick, private conversation with LeBron made news.If you care about such things, here are the latest…

items.title: Austin Reaves is ready to step into whatever role JJ Redick asks this season
items.summary: Los Angeles, CA - February 20: Guard Austin Reaves #15 of the Los Angeles Lakers drives to the basket against center Yanic Konan Niederhauser #14 of the LA Clippers in the first half of a NBA basketball game at Crypto.com Arena in Los Angeles on Friday, February 20, 2026. (Photo by Keith Birmingham/MediaNews Group/Pasadena Star-News via Getty Images) | MediaNews Group via Getty Images The Lakers will look a lot different when they take the floor for the 2026-27 season. Quentin Grimes, Collin Sexton, Sandro Mamukelashvili and Walker Kessler are Lakers. Marcus Smart, Luke Kennard, Rui Hachimura and LeBron James are no longer part of the franchise.Though much has changed, one thing the Lakers can still rely on is Austin Reaves. He remains with the Lakers and is now unequivocally the No. 2 option.Throughout his career, whether he’s coming off the bench, starting, scoring in bunches, or…

items.title: The Year of the Rat-line
items.summary: Year of the Rat-line: You are optimistic, gullible, and prone to make poor decisions with money. You’re most compatible with the Diamondbacks and the Cardinals; avoid the Lakers and the Spurs.38.5? Seems low. A little too low. The brains at FanDuel must have blown a gasket when it comes to the Phoenix Suns’ win projection.  Or maybe they tried to replace their brains with AI, and it hallucinated a team that didn’t just move Grayson Allen and Royce O’Neale (and a first-round pick) for Miles Bridges and Luke Kennard.  Maybe they didn’t see Khaman go a cold 40% from three-point range in the Summer League. Maybe they missed the Brightside press releases during free agency, when Gregory sealed his key free agents like envelopes. First Collin, then Goodie, then Mark. Suns fans are going to start this season screaming 50 wins. I’ll be right there with them. How do the Suns…

items.title: Ex-Lakers star Pau Gasol cheers on home country at World Cup final
items.summary: Just moments before the World Cup final between Spain and Argentina was set to kick off, a Lakers champion revealed he is at the game.Pau Gasol posted a photo of himself and his wife, Catherine McDonnell, at the game during pregame festivities.Gasol, a native of Spain, is one of the many celebrities and former top athletes of the world watching the biggest soccer match of the year between Spain and Argentina.Gasol is an avid soccer fan and has been a huge FC Barcelona fan. He has long admired Lionel Messi and has called him a “special player.”Pau Gasol spotted at FIFA World Cup final. IMAGN IMAGES via ReutersThroughout this year’s tournament, Gasol has been active in watching it and has raved about watching Messi, Kylian Mbappe and Erling Haaland.Recently, Gasol made a major investment in soccer by investing 55 millionEuros ($62.7 million) into FC Barcelona’s top-flight women’s soccer…

items.title: Karl-Anthony Towns talks Knicks, Call of Duty, and why he’s already chasing another championship
items.summary: “I feel like we got something else to go win”. That was Karl-Anthony Towns’ response when Mark Grigsby, the studio head of Infinity Ward, asked him how it felt to be a champion. At a recent Call of Duty Modern Warfare 4 event that took place in a private section of the Flatiron restaurant, Aqua Roma, Towns, who’s long been known to be a Call of Duty player, was a special guest. Towns spoke glowingly about the franchise and how he grew up playing the games all the time. Towns also revealed that he used to play a lot with his close friend, Devin Booker, and also shouted out former teammate Anthony Edwards, with whom he also used to play often.When Tyler Bahl, the chief marketing officer at Activision, asked the big man who’s between him and the guys he mentioned were better, he answered, “Right now, D-Book,” before jokingly adding, “We were busy trying to win a championship,” to which the…

items.title: Thunder deal Lu Dort to Hawks in three-team trade that sends Zaccharie Risacher to Mavericks
items.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder’s cost-cutting measures continue. Although this time, the Thunder are moving an integral piece to their contendership, and that’s all-world defender Lu Dort, who has been with the organization since signing as an undrafted rookie in 2019. Dort is being traded from OKC to the Atlanta Hawks in a three-team deal, per Shams Charania of ESPN.The deal will also see the Hawks send three second-round picks to the Thunder, as well as selling off 2024 No. 1 overall pick Zaccharie Risacher to the Dallas Mavericks, who are sending Ryan Nembhard — younger brother of Indiana Pacer guard Andrew Nembhard — to Atlanta.Luguentz Dort celebrates during the game against the San Antonio Spurs during Game 7 of the NBA Western Conference Finals on May 30, 2026, at Paycom Center in Oklahoma City. NBAE via Getty ImagesOKC, which also traded Isaiah Joe to the Detroit Pistons and Aaron…
